80.reason: [This condition is flawed because it assumes declarations end when the second lookahead token is LPARENT, but this doesn't correctly identify function definitions vs main function. It may cause incorrect parsing when there are no parameters or when tokens are exhausted.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is flawed because it assumes declarations end when the second lookahead token is LPARENT, but this doesn't correctly identify function definitions vs main function. It may cause incorrect parsing when there are no parameters or when tokens are exhausted.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect because it assumes that any token followed by '(' indicates the end of declarations, but 'main' function starts with 'int main(' and there could be variable declarations like 'int a;' before function definitions. Also, peek(2) may cause null pointer issues when there aren't enough tokens ahead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly assumes that any token sequence without '(' at position 2 must be a declaration, but fails to handle MainFuncDef which starts with 'int' 'main' and should be parsed separately]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly assumes that any declaration must not have '(' two tokens ahead, but fails to handle the MainFuncDef case which starts with 'int' 'main' '(' and should be parsed separately. Also, peek(2) may return null causing NullPointerException.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect because it assumes that any declaration is followed by a token that has LPARENT two positions ahead, but MainFuncDef starts with "int main()" where "int" is at current position and "main" is next, so LPARENT is at peek(2). This causes the parser to incorrectly treat MainFuncDef as a VarDecl.]
